NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 6th September, 2000 GSE 708(E).-~In exercise of the powers asnferrsd by clause (g) of Sufo-teioii <2) of section 60 and section 198 read with clause (a) of Sab-section (2) of section 200 of the Railways Act, 1989 (24 of 1989), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Indian Railways (Open Lines) General Rules, 1976, namely:— I (1) These rules may be called the Indian Railways (Open Lmes)General (Second Amendment) Rules, 2000. (2) They shall come into ibrce or* the date of their publication in the Official Gazette, 2. In the Indian Railways (Open Lines) General Rules, 1976, in Chapter in, in rule 3.38 :— (i) the provision consisting of clauses (a), (b) and (c) shall be numbered as sub-rule (1); (ii) after sub-rule (1) so numbered, Use following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely :— "(2) When a runtwng line is blocked by a stabled load, wagon, vechicle or by a train which is to cross or give precedence to another train or immediately after the arrival of a train at the station the points in rear on double line sections and at either end on single line sections should be immediately set against the blocked line except when shunting or any other movement is required to be done immediately in tliat direction on that line." (No. 99/Safery (A&R)/19/6] SHANTINARAIN, Member Traffic and Ex-Offido Secy. Note:—The principal rules-were published vide notification number GSR 445(E), dated the 21st July, 1981 and subsequently amended Notification number :— (1) GSR 320, dated the 16th, April, 1983, (2) GSR 352, dated the'30th April, 1983, &){ GSR 514(E), dated the 27th-June, 1983, (4) GSR 476(E), dated the 28th June, 1984, <S> GSR 245, dated the 23rd May, 1992, (6) GSR 83, dated the 17th February, 1996, (7) GSR 101, dated the 23rd May, 1998, (8) GSR 47, dated the i 3th February, 1999, (9) GSR 213(E), dated the 1 8th March, 1999, (10) GSR 283(E), dated the 26th April, 1999, and (11) GSR 581(E), dated the 3rd My, 2000.
